name: ETH 4h Compression Fakeout Re-Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: compression breaks one way, loses acceptance, reclaims fast value, and then re-expands in the opposite direction with directional confirmation
thesis: ETH 4h is 100% chop in the regime snapshot, which makes it the best lane in the basket for a fakeout-to-reexpansion concept. The edge is not breakout prediction; it is betting that trapped breakout positioning unwinds once price reclaims value and directional pressure flips.
invalidation_condition: If fakeouts remain too sparse, or if value reclaim after the fakeout does not generate enough opposite-direction expansion to clear costs, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Volatility compresses, price pokes one way, loses that direction, reclaims fast value, and expands the other way as the failed side unwinds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop tends to produce failed initial breaks. ETH 4h is cleaner than TAO for seeing whether reclaim-after-fakeout can launch a tradable second move with better asymmetry than chasing the first breakout.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because the compression structure is clearer than AXS and less noisy than TAO, while still sitting in the precise regime that should generate fakeouts.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: value reclaim line after failed break

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The initial break may simply be the real move, making the reclaim bar noise and the reverse entry structurally wrong.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the idea trades but follow-through is weak, strengthen the directional handoff requirement before loosening the squeeze threshold.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Moderate trade count with some quick stop-outs on true breakouts, but strong asymmetry on authentic fakeout unwind moves.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h shows non-zero fakeout participation with positive second-leg expansion, and one of TAO or AXS preserves the same unwind grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trades, or reclaim bars that repeatedly fail to develop into meaningful opposite-direction expansion.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but broader than prior squeeze families because it trades the reclaim-and-handoff sequence rather than exact persistence chains

test_lanes.why_included: Best chop lane in the basket for fakeout-to-reexpansion behavior.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough squeeze fakeouts that reclaim and opposite-direction handoff create real post-cost moves.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h either trends too cleanly or fakeouts stay too sparse to matter.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with occasional strong unwind runners.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same fakeout unwind becomes larger on a more expressive lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h keeps the same reclaim-and-handoff sequence with acceptable structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h turns fakeouts into chaos rather than structured unwind moves.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count than ETH but bigger second legs.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism survives a thinner structurally different asset.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS 4h preserves the fakeout unwind pattern with enough density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS becomes noise with no stable reclaim behavior.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Messier but potentially denser fakeouts than ETH.
